seoTitle: How to Call Another Workflow in n8n With Execute Workflow
metaDescription: Learn how to use n8n's Execute Workflow node to trigger a separate workflow, pass data between them, and build modular automations.
intro: As your n8n automations grow, splitting logic into separate, reusable workflows keeps things manageable, and the Execute Workflow node makes that possible. This guide covers adding the node, selecting a target workflow, and passing data between the parent and child pipelines.

steps.heading: Add the Execute Workflow node
steps.body: From your parent workflow canvas, search for and add the Execute Workflow node where you want the sub-workflow to run.

steps.heading: Select your target workflow
steps.body: Choose the specific child workflow you want to trigger from your list of existing workflows.

steps.heading: Map your data payload
steps.body: Pass any variables or JSON data the child workflow needs from the parent workflow's upstream nodes.

steps.heading: Configure execution behavior
steps.body: Decide whether the parent workflow should wait for the child workflow to finish and return data, or continue immediately.

steps.heading: Test the connection
steps.body: Run the workflow to confirm data passes correctly between the parent and child pipelines.

faq.question: What node do I use to call another workflow in n8n?
faq.answer: The Execute Workflow node is used to trigger a separate workflow from within your current one.

faq.question: Can I pass data into the workflow I'm calling?
faq.answer: Yes, you can map upstream data into a JSON payload that the child workflow receives.

faq.question: Does the parent workflow wait for the child to finish?
faq.answer: This depends on your configuration, since you can choose whether to wait for the sub-workflow's output or continue immediately.

faq.question: Why would I split workflows instead of using one big workflow?
faq.answer: Splitting logic into reusable workflows makes automations easier to maintain, test, and reuse across different projects.
